behaviors.description: The generated reply references specific points from the incoming email rather than producing a generic response.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "generated reply references specific points ... rather than producing a generic response." This is about LLM output quality and specificity, which a stubbed model would hide. Not Workflow: behavior asks for one generation quality decision, not a multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run with a real LLM on representative real email threads; assert the draft explicitly grounds to thread-specific points (e.g., named commitments/questions) and fails when output is generic.

behaviors.description: The factual consistency check flags claims in the draft that contradict entries in the knowledge base.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "flags claims in the draft that contradict entries in the knowledge base." Failure boundary is checker + claim extraction + KB lookup handoff across modules. Integration-brake: (a) no explicit OS/network/subprocess requirement, (b) no explicit requirement that real AI/ML judgment quality be validated, (c) no multi-agent chain. Rejected higher level: behavior text does not require real model output or real external infra.
behaviors.test_description: Wire draft parser, contradiction-check service, and KB repository together (in-process/local DB). Feed drafts with known contradictory claims and verify contradiction flags propagate with correct claim references.

behaviors.description: When the knowledge base has no relevant entries, the draft includes a disclaimer rather than fabricating facts.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "draft includes a disclaimer rather than fabricating facts." This validates generation behavior under missing-knowledge conditions; mocked outputs would miss hallucination/fabrication failures. Not Workflow: single generation decision under one condition, not a multi-step agentic pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Use real LLM calls with prompts/threads where retrieval returns no relevant KB entries; assert draft contains required disclaimer language and does not introduce unsupported factual claims.

behaviors.description: The approval workflow blocks sending until the user explicitly approves — no draft is sent automatically.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "blocks sending until the user explicitly approves — no draft is sent automatically." This is state handoff/orchestration between draft state, approval gate, and sender module. Integration-brake: (a) behavior does not require real SMTP/API transport semantics, (b) no dependence on model output quality, (c) no multi-agent sequence. Rejected System: no behavior phrase requiring real external email infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Wire workflow controller + persistence + send adapter (test double). Verify send is never invoked before explicit approval event and is invoked only after approval transition.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — read thread, generate draft, check facts, present for approval — works end-to-end for a real email thread.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "full sequence ... works end-to-end for a real email thread." and explicit multi-stage chain "read thread, generate draft, check facts, present for approval." This is a full agent pipeline with at least one real agentic generation step and stage-to-stage handoff correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end scenario on a real/staging email thread: ingest thread, call real LLM for draft, run fact-check stage, surface approval UI/state, and verify outputs and gating across all stages.
